hindi: वन्दे ऽहं शीतलां देवीं रासभस्थां दिगम्बराम्।
english: I bow to Goddess Shitala, mounted on a donkey, sky-clad
roman: Vande aham Shitalaam devim rasabha stham digambaram

english: Holding a broom and a pot, with head adorned by a winnowing basket
roman: Marjanee kalasho petam surpa lankruta mastakam

english: O cool Goddess Shitala, destroyer of all fevers
roman: Shitale Shitale Devi sarva jvara vinashini

english: Destroyer of disease and sin, remover of all ailments
roman: Roga papa nihantri cha sarvaaamaya nivarini

english: O Shitala fond of neem leaves, beautiful on your donkey seat
roman: Neem parna priye Shitale Gardabha sana Shobhane

english: Worshipped on Shitala Ashtami, remover of all diseases — salutations
roman: Shitala ashtami pujita sarva roga hare namah

english: Whoever reads this Shitala Ashtakam daily with purity
roman: Shitala ashtakam idam yah pathet nityam shuchirnara

english: Removes all inauspicious conditions for children and wards off all diseases
roman: Baala arishtam haratyeva sarva rogam nivarayet
